title: Infosys Knowledge Institute Releases Responsible AI Research Report
detailed_summary: Infosys Knowledge Institute released findings from its Responsible Enterprise AI in the Agentic Era report. The research surveyed over 1,500 business executives and interviewed 40 senior decision-makers, finding broad AI-related incident exposure and a gap between responsible AI adoption and readiness. The announcement is primarily thought leadership and does not disclose Infosys financial results, guidance, deals, or corporate actions.

key_facts.fact: Infosys Knowledge Institute unveiled insights from the report Responsible Enterprise AI in the Agentic Era.
The report surveyed over 1,500 business executives and interviewed 40 senior decision-makers across Australia, France, Germany, UK, US, and New Zealand.
The findings state that 78% of companies see RAI as a business growth driver, while only 2% have adequate RAI controls in place to safeguard against reputational risk and financial loss.

important_text_spans.text: while 78% of companies see RAI as a business growth driver, only 2% have adequate RAI controls in place to safeguard against reputational risk and financial loss.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the central finding of the Infosys Knowledge Institute report.

important_text_spans.text: Infosys used an anonymous format to conduct an online survey of 1,502 business executives
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the scale of the research methodology.

search_keywords: Responsible Enterprise AI in the Agentic Era
sample_questions: What did Infosys Knowledge Institute report about responsible AI readiness in August 2025?
